reviews.review_text:
I'm a big fan of Far Cry series but... 

I lost my old ubisoft account and now I can't play the game anymore bcs it's said it's linked to my old account. Been asking here and there to the Ubisoft support but doesn't resolve my problem. 

This game is in my Steam library but I can't even access it. Now if I want to play this game I have to purchase the same game in my new ubisoft account. What a waste? 

And it seems many also having the same problem. If only y'all can solve this problem!

* Played back in 2016, 10 years later still looks great
* Probably one of the best games in the series
* The buzzer copter is just excellent still
* Buzzer Copter + Grenade launcher OMG!
* Some real thumping weapons 
* The bow is special 
* Outposts never get tiresome to take over
* 95/100

*Buys game
*Asks me to make an account
*Tries
*Sorry, try again later

"You have to do everything yourself these days"
-Joker from the Dark Knight

Trying to get Far Cry 4 co-op to work with friends is a nightmare, I can't even connect without a VPN and even when I do, it’s only a matter of time before it crashes, although it will work just fine with randoms.

This game is a massive improvement over Far Cry 3 gameplay-wise (I'm a diehard fan of FC3). The game in 2026 is more than playable and interesting. I personally received more joy playing this game than Cyberpunk (Yes, I'm dead-ass serious). But ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥... How bad the story and characters are... The story is so stupid and nonsensical - the main character just does things and makes decisions only because the story was written that way. The MC's motivation doesn't work for the player, the characters are boring and grey, and Pagan is not even a weak version of Vaas charisma-wise (still better than 90% of antagonists tho)...

Gameplay + World + Joy - 9/10, Story + Missions + Characters - 3/10, Overall - 6.5/10.
this is basically far cry 3 but it's set in asia and you get to side with 2 different leaders, lowkey mid but it's still fire and adds a bunch of things that far cry 3 was missing.

Although this game is not a masterpiece of storytelling, but it’s an absolute blast to play. It’s the kind of game you boot up when you want freedom, chaos, and a world that constantly surprises you. If you love open-world shooters where anything can happen, this one is worth experiencing—even today.

Far cry 4 is a good game, definitely has some jank in it and also showing its age but its still fun. leaving negative review for ubisoft connect.

Looking forward to the day ubisoft goes under or is bought out then being unable to play any of their games, i hope this is the last time i spend money on anything ubisoft.

It's impossible to play this s... Ubisoft requires you to have an account on Ubisoft Connect, and it simply doesn't work, you are asked to select captchas forever. I will NEVER buy anything other game from Ubisoft.

overall pretty neat game. visuals still look stunning 12 years later, if you enjoyed far cry 3 there is a high chance you'll enjoy this one. loads more activities to do and existing ones that were in 3 have been enhanced. if you like achievement hunting high chance you wont like it because there are co op achievements

Very good graphics for a 2014 game, good gunplay, a ton of options for stealth, and even more options for going loud, because who needs a suppressor when you can lob grenades from a helicopter. Story overall is decent but isn't anything to write home about, though it presents some interesting choices and moral dilemmas. Game has a lot of content overall, including the campaign, various side missions and additional activities, and even collectibles if anyone cares about those. Far Cry 4 can be anything from a stealth game to a Terminator simulator. If you find yourself getting bored, there's always ways to spice things up like mastering an outpost with only a knife, taking on a fortress the loud way with naught but a bow, or just saying "♥♥♥♥ it" and riding an elephant into an outpost. When on sale (which it often is, nowadays) I'd consider Far Cry 4 a great deal, and one of the best in the series. It may not be as good as Far Cry 3, but it sure is better than 5 and 6, and I personally prefer 4 to 3. Worth mentioning that 4 holds up MUCH better than 3 visually in 2026, if that matters to anyone.
One of the few games where I empathized with the antagonist and felt sorry for him. Overall, it’s a solid Far Cry game, 8/10.
Wanted to pick the achievements and spend a chill evening but it requests an activation key so I can't even open the game

Could not play the game because ubisoft forces you to create a stupid ubisoft connect account . but whenever i tried to create an account, i got the error message "error try again later". i tried on the pos program that got installed in my computer alongside the game , i tried it on their webpage on 3 different browsers , and i tried on my cellphone , for a whole day , try again later. i said eff this bs, i rather spend my money on games that i can play. tell whoever is left in ubisoft to just get rid off that trash launcher . i will not be purchasing any ubisoft games because of this.

I bought this game back in 2021, and it took me almost 5 years to finally finish it xD. It was just sitting in my library all this time, and honestly, I regret not playing it earlier. I finally completed the campaign, unlocked achievements, and experienced the story. Now only the Valley of the Yetis DLC remains, which I’ll be playing in the future.

The story, graphics, characters, and music were all amazing. As someone from India, I really appreciate how well Ubisoft researched the culture and setting to make the game feel authentic. The NPCs randomly abusing in Hindi always made me laugh 😭. There’s even a particular mission narrated completely in Hindi, and I honestly appreciate that because very few games include proper Hindi dialogue in AAA games.

The songs played in vehicles are also really good, and many of them got stuck in my head long after closing the game.

I completed Far Cry 5 before playing this, so at first the graphics felt a bit downgraded, but that was my mistake for playing FC5 first. Even then, this game still holds up really well in 2026. The Himalayan setting is absolutely beautiful and gives the game a unique vibe.

Far Cry games are known for their villains, and this game definitely didn’t disappoint. Pagan Min has officially entered my list of favorite video game villains.

The only thing I didn’t like was the lack of a New Game+ feature like newer Ubisoft games have. But considering this game released back in 2014, it’s understandable. Still it would be amazing if Ubisoft added an NG+ mode someday so players could replay the story with all the weapons and abilities they unlocked.

Far Cry 4 also made me wish Ubisoft would create a proper Assassin’s Creed game set in India someday. The culture, atmosphere, and history have so much potential, just like how Assassin’s Creed Origins explored Ancient Egypt and Assassin’s Creed Odyssey explored Ancient Greece. I’m still hoping Ubisoft explores India in the future.

Overall, I was very late to play this game, but better late than never xD. Even after all these years, Far Cry 4 still feels special and is definitely a must try-game for every Far Cry fan.
Wish I could've just joined Pagan. Fair warning for people wanting to 100% this game the multiplayer is giga dead :(

do not buy this game expecaly if you are on a computer it glitches and saves doesnt work
when you your pc dies with it

Such a great game, It uses the esthetic Of the Himalaya region and the beautiful nature in India in such a good way.

This is definitely my favorite Far Cry game.
really good game like skyrim but modern and no dragons
I have played FAR CRY franchise games at least 3-4 times and everytime it seems like new game to play for me. Enjoyable!!!!!!

The perfect refinement of the Far Cry formula in a gorgeous setting!

Far Cry 4 took everything that made its predecessor amazing and polished it to absolute perfection. The snowy Himalayan mountains of Kirat are stunningly beautiful, packed with verticality, and feel incredibly alive. The new villain, Pagan Min, is phenomenal—he is flamboyant, deeply complex, and steals every single scene he is in.

The gameplay upgrades in this entry make the sandbox combat so much more chaotic and fun. Being able to ride elephants into enemy outposts, use a grappling hook to scale massive cliffs, fly a buzzer, and instantly deploy the wingsuit gives you total tactical freedom. The gunplay feels extremely punchy, and the addition of co-op and the Arena mode added hours of extra enjoyment for me.

It is a fantastic, action-packed adventure that delivers non-stop entertainment. For me, it is easily one of the best and most polished open-world shooters out there.

Firstly, the game is stunning. Now coming to gameplay, it is quite identical to its predecessor, similar types of missions, animal hunts, outposts with addition of some fortresses. But I did not enjoy this game's hallucinogenic missions similar to it's predecessor. In general it is a Far Cry game with improved visuals and exploration.

Was a great entry to the title. I went back to play it for the achievements, and I really enjoyed it. Unfortunately you can't get them all since it has several multiplayer achievements, and the multiplayer is essentially dead on PC. But still worth playing the game.

Very cool and stealth-shooting game. I like it very much, exspecially Far Cry 3 with crazy villain Vaas Montenegro. Pagan Min is some sort of bad copy of Vaas. As much as you play this game, the more you get addictive by crafting things, upgrading skills, selling stuff to merchants or in safe house and buying many different weapons. And I also love to see different places in game like here, where you're in Indian village called Kryat. Still better than USA places like Los Angeles, Los Santos, Boston, Chicago, etc. This is something different to see and explore. Great game!

I really did try to give this game a chance. But i can't bring myself to finish this, It is just getting so repetitive and I don't give a crap about the story and its 20 min long unskippable cutscenes that have bored my ass to tears.
I love the game. Just behind Far cry 5 in the series for me but why is there achievements for the online pvp game? That ♥♥♥♥♥ dead. Literally waited 20 minutes in queue for absolutely nothing.

What I Liked:
1. The sandbox nature of the game. You can use any vehicle, any gun, and any approach for your missions. Only a very few missions require you to be stealthy, and even then, it is only for a small section.
2. Pagan Min, the main villain, is a very interesting character. The other supporting characters, such as Sabal and Amita, are also well written.
3. Rabi Ray Rana (or Radio Free Kyrat) is funny to listen to at times. The songs played on the radio are also very nice.
4. The environment is really pretty to look at, and the inclusion of a chopper to fly around in is the best decision.

What I Disliked:
1. The usual Ubisoft collectibles and copy-pasted, dumb side activities.
2. Nothing other than the main missions is properly mo-capped.
3. It tries a bit too hard to be like Far Cry 3.

Far Cry 4 is a stunning, action-packed ride that perfectly refines the chaotic sandbox formula of its predecessor. Set against the breathtaking, snow-capped peaks of the Himalayas, the game is visually gorgeous and creates an incredibly immersive atmosphere. The main story is nice. We are here in Kyrat to fulfil our mother's last wish. Spending a month clearing the main campaign and tackling almost all of the side missions never felt like a chore, thanks to the addictive loop of liberating outposts, grappling up sheer cliffs, and dealing with Kyrat's unpredictable wildlife. The Honey Badger is menacing. While the story beats can occasionally feel a bit familiar if you played the previous entry, the verticality of the mountain setting and the sheer variety of ways to approach every combat encounter make it an absolute blast from start to finish. If you're looking for a gorgeous open-world playground where you can wingsuit off a Himalayan peak, then Kyrat is absolutely worth the trip.
It is the best far cry game, very unique, sooooo fun

y'all make good games Ubisoft but running this game on PC is a real struggle. Ubisoft connect keeps crashing if I run the game on steam so if I want my achievements to register properly I have to run the game on Ubisoft connect. and this game struggles with modern CPUs, the fps stuttering makes the game ALMOST unplayable, even with a 3rd gen CPU that meets the system requirements it still runs like ♥♥♥♥. you can fix this issue via task manager but you'll have to do it every time you start the game which many ppl forget until their game starts crying

Its a good game but incredibly broken, FC3 has crashes but runs perfectly, this game never crashes but has so many issues you wish it did, it basically REQUIRES the Golden Path modpack to get a playable experience, you also have to go into task manager and change affinity everytime you launch it, and if you ahve a 16:10 monitor you get black bars at top and bottom which you cant get rid of without risking getting banned (the only fix is a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ exe that anticheats dont like)

Far Cry 3 and 4 are absolutely peak Far Cry to me. The story modes are amazing, the open world is fun to explore, and the gameplay never really gets boring. Far Cry 5 and 6 are alright but they feel a little choppy compared to these older ones. Everything about Far Cry 4 just feels right and it’s definitely worth buying if you like open world games.

the game is incredible, don't get me wrong, but the minute you open the level editor, the entire thing falls apart. 56 hours of gameplay inaccessible because of some stupid bug. it's pretty frustrating. i am sure there is a solution, and i will keep searching for one, as i want to finish the game.
A continuation of the success of the previous game in a unique setting that rarely gets used.
one of the best fps games and second best in far cry series

Far Cry 4 takes everything that made Far Cry 3 great and expands it into a larger, more chaotic, and visually stunning open-world experience. Set in the fictional Himalayan country of Kyrat, the game delivers explosive action, memorable characters, and endless freedom in how you approach missions.

One of the strongest parts of the game is its villain, Pagan Min. Charismatic, unpredictable, and strangely entertaining, he steals every scene he appears in. Even though he isn’t constantly present throughout the story, his personality leaves a lasting impression and makes the narrative far more engaging.

Gameplay is where Far Cry 4 truly shines. Whether you prefer stealth, sniping enemies from a distance, going in with heavy weapons, or simply causing chaos with explosives and wildlife, the game gives you complete freedom. The wingsuit, grappling hook, vehicles, and huge arsenal make exploration and combat consistently fun.

Kyrat itself is a beautiful and dangerous open world. Snow-covered mountains, dense forests, villages, and temples create a strong atmosphere, while random events like animal attacks and enemy ambushes keep the world feeling alive.

However, the game does feel very similar to Far Cry 3 in structure. Many side activities become repetitive over time, and the story choices don’t always have meaningful consequences. Some missions also follow the usual Ubisoft open-world formula too closely.

Pros
Beautiful open world with strong atmosphere
Excellent freedom in combat and exploration
Memorable villain in Pagan Min
Fun weapons, vehicles, and traversal mechanics
Entertaining open-world chaos
Cons
Repetitive side content
Very similar to Far Cry 3 in design
Story choices lack major impact

Overall, Far Cry 4 is an incredibly fun open-world shooter with fantastic gameplay and a memorable setting. Even years after release, it remains one of the most enjoyable entries in the Far Cry franchise.

Rating: 8.5/10 🔥

Far Cry 4 is honestly one of the most fun open world games you can ever play. Kyrat is a really cool setting and there’s always something happening while exploring the map. One minute you’re doing a mission and the next you’re getting chased by animals or running into enemies randomly.
The gameplay feels great, especially the combat and taking over outposts. It gives you a lot of freedom to play however you want, whether that’s going in stealthy or setting everything on fire.
And I would like to say is Pagan Min is probably one of the best villains in the Far Cry series at least in my opinion. The game also supports Co-op, if you have friend you want troll or cause chaos, I strongly recommend you play it with a friend.
Definitely worth playing if you like open world shooters.

I like it for the most part, but it's unfinished in my opinion.  You can unlock the northern part of the country by completing missions, but it's obvious Ubisoft didn't put as much effort into it as they did for the southern part of Kyrat.  If you're looking to try the Far Cry franchise, get Primal instead - it's a more polished game.
this game is one of the best from Far Cry franchise, recommend 100%, also does not need crazy system to run and low graphics still look so good.

I played this game right after finishing Far Cry 3 and its DLCs, and I’ve got to say, it’s an absolute blast. In my opinion, it’s better than FC3 in almost every way: gameplay mechanics, physics, and even the overall story.

The only areas where Far Cry 3 still has the edge are the DLCs and the main villain. Pagan Min is slightly less insane than Vaas and is portrayed as a more tactical and calculated antagonist. Because of that, he barely appears in missions until the very end. While that can feel a bit disappointing, it also makes sense given the kind of character he’s supposed to be.

The game itself is packed with content and side activities, making it easy to sink a lot of hours into. One personal gripe I have, though, is the enemy accuracy, these guys basically have Crazy aimbots. Enemies can spot and laser you from absurd distances with assault rifles, which can get pretty annoying at times.

DLCs

Escape from Durgesh Prison:
This was by far the most frustrating DLC for me, I had to take multiple breaks to complete it. It basically feels like an enemy rush mode with only a very thin connection to the main story. Personally, I didn’t enjoy it much.

Valley of the Yetis:
Now this is a fantastic DLC. It’s fast-paced, has a decent plot, and is genuinely fun throughout. While I still don’t think it reaches the level of Far Cry 3: Blood Dragon, it’s definitely enjoyable enough to keep you invested.

Overall, this was absolutely worth the purchas, especially on discount. I had a great time with it, and unlike Far Cry 3 on Steam, I didn’t experience any crashing issues on my laptop. I’d definitely recommend it to anyone who enjoys fun open world games.
Yeah this game is actually amazing but not better than the fifth one we waiting for 7 pls ubisoft or i fart

Okay, first off I must admit, I am a huge fan of what I consider to be the core Far Cry Trilogy (3, 4 and 5). But in my personal opinion, 4 has the best gameplay of the three, and that really owes to the incredibly diverse selection of weapons that you can unlock by game's end. Each and every one of them handles and fires in a unique way. And the Shanath Arena Mini-Games are absolutely great, and none of the rest of the series has anything even close to comparing to that. Plus, Ajay Ghale? He's a complex, relatable, human feeling character, who you genuinely want to see achieve his goals in Kyrat. 4 also has beautiful graphics, being set in the real-life beautiful Himalaya Mountains. The hybrid Indian-Tibetan-Chinese temples and ruins and whatnot throughout are very well designed and interesting and pleasing to look at as well. I would give this game overall a 8/10, and I would go so far as to call it the greatest First Person Shooter ever made.

[h3]A little bit of everything - and it's (almost) all good[/h3]

I came into this game having previously played Far Cry 3, and I found 4 to be essentially the same game with a different setting and characters, plus a few extra mission types. I don't consider that to be a fault as I greatly enjoyed 3 and was happy to play more of it, but those players looking for a game with a distinct personality may wish to look elsewhere

Once again we have a typical Ubisoft-style kitchen sink approach, with combat, stealth, vehicles, crafting, vendors, player choices as to which faction to support, lots of different side missions, a big map to gradually reveal via climbing towers, and approximately 1,000,000 collectibles cluttering up said map. I was happy to play with all of it, although by about halfway through I just wanted to get on with it and finish. That didn't stop me from 100 percenting the game though, so clearly I was enjoying myself.

I found the villain and other NPCs a little less interesting than in FC3, and I thought that the setting being far more identifiable with a real location (it's pretty clearly Nepal, more or less) caused some discomfort, just because I don't like equating a real place with some magical playground where I can run around and kill people.

My one real quibble was that there was just too much random combat. I would be getting into 3-4 pitched battles just to get to my mission location. In FC3, as you cleared camps the enemy would generally no longer appear in that area. It was nice to feel that you were having an effect on the world, and could relax and enjoy the scenery as you made your way through the area. This version, not so much (you can clear fortresses which supposedly reduces the pressure but I didn't notice much of a difference).

If you like an action/adventure game that is maybe a bit generic but has everything anyone could want, check this out. If you've played Ubisoft games before, you already know what you're getting.

PS - there are multiplayer components to this game, and you will have to get into them if you want to get every achievement. Fortunately, the MP servers are still up and there is a friendly, helpful community that is more than happy to help you get them. This is almost as much a recommendation for the game as the game itself!

Doesn't require a beefy PC to play, and you can usually find it at a discount on Steam. If it's cheap enough, get a copy for a friend as well and have some fun messing with the NPCs.

I ultimately knew what I was paying for, a semi-difficult open world tactical shooter about liberating outposts and making morally grey choices; a pretty standard Far Cry experience.

What I didn't expect was the beautiful worldbuilding and love Ubisoft Montreal put into making Kyrat and it's landscapes, nor the actual depth put into Pagan Min, Ajay Ghale, and the rest of the characters.

The best way to describe Kyrat is breathtaking, an expansive Himalayan valley full of shrines, villages, and the occasional outpost. I probably spent around 5 hours of my playtime just driving around the map and taking in the scenery Ubisoft had put into the world, the game still brilliantly holding up even 12 years after its release.

The story itself is simple yet complex; 
You are Ajay Ghale, son of Mohan Ghale who founded the rebel group called the Golden Path. Your mother, Ishwari Ghale had died recently and wished for her ashes to be spread in Kyrat at the shrine to her deceased newborn, Lakshmana.

In that quest you get yourself mixed up with the modern Golden Path's power struggle and liberation of Kyrat from Pagan Min. With it all leading up with you having to confront Pagan at his palace to finally fulfill your mother's last wishes.

I highly recommend to play the game yourself because spoiling anything would honestly ruin some twists that I wasn't expecting myself

Now as much as I loved this game, I found the gameplay lacking at parts. Where level design and mechanics failed to make the game enjoyable or fun for the player; but these were few and far between.
